items.label: spec-compiler
messages: Now let me check for the baseline and get the git SHA:
Now I have everything I need. Let me compose the acceptance contract:
Now let me validate the YAML:
All validations pass. Now let me commit:
Commit successful. Now let me call `report_outcome`:

The acceptance contract for `product-quick-view` has been compiled and committed. Here's a summary of what was produced:  **`acceptance.yml` contents:**  - **2 test fixtures**: `plp-multi-color` (`/category/mens-accessories` with multi-color swatch assertion) and `plp-add-to-bag` (`/category/womens-clothing-dresses` with in-stock assertion) - **9 required DOM elements**: `quick-view-trigger` (many), `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-modal-error`, `quick-view-add-to-cart-btn`, `quick-view-view-full-details-link`, `quick-view-modal-close-btn`, `add-to-cart-modal`, `product-view`, `inventory-message` - **6 required flows**: open-quick-view-from-tile, switch-color-swatch-in-quick-view, add-to-bag-from-quick-view, close-quick-view-restores-focus, no-pickup-ui-in-quick-view, add-to-bag-disabled-when-unavailable - **3 forbidden network failures**: products, baskets creation, basket items endpoints - **6 base template reuse entries**: ProductView, ProductTile, useProductViewModal, useAddToCartModalContext, useCurrentBasket, useShopperBasketsMutation  URLs were resolved without locale/site prefixes per `config/default.js` (`url.locale: 'none'`, `url.site: 'none'`). All multi-instance testids (`quick-view-trigger`) use `match: first` in flow steps per Strategy A.

items.label: baseline-analyzer
messages: Now I have the inputs. Let me identify the targets: - **PLP 1**: `/category/mens-accessories` (used in 4 flows) - **PLP 2**: `/category/womens-clothing-dresses` (used in 2 flows) - **Modal**: `quick-view-trigger` — this is the feature being built, won't exist yet  Let me capture baseline errors on both PLP pages using Playwright.
Good, the page loaded. I can see 2 console errors. Let me capture the full console messages, then scroll to trigger lazy errors, and also check network requests.
Now let me scroll the page, check network requests, then navigate to the second PLP.
Good. Now let me navigate to the second PLP page.
Now let me scroll and get network failures for the second page.
I now have all the data I need from both pages. The error patterns are consistent across both PLPs. Let me now produce the baseline.json.
Now let me validate the JSON and commit.
Baseline committed successfully. Let me report the outcome.

Baseline analysis complete. Here's a summary:  **Targets captured (3):** - `PLP-mens-accessories` → `/category/mens-accessories` (page) - `PLP-womens-dresses` → `/category/womens-clothing-dresses` (page)   - `Quick View modal` → trigger testid `quick-view-trigger` (modal — not yet implemented)  **Findings:** | Channel | Count | Persistent | Transient | |---|---|---|---| | Console errors | 12 entries | 8 (tagged) | 4 (untagged) | | Network failures | 7 entries | 2 (tagged) | 5 (untagged) | | Uncaught exceptions | 0 | — | — |  **Key persistent patterns (platform noise):** 1. `getServerSnapshot should be cached` — framework-warning (React internals) 2. `defaultProps will be removed` — legacy-deprecation (React 19 prep) 3. `ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED` at `c360a.salesforce.com` — network-sandbox (DataCloud DNS) 4. `use-datacloud._handleApiError` — network-sandbox (DataCloud event API) 5. `r: 403 Forbidden` — network-sandbox (shopper-context/configurations sandbox 403s)

items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now I have enough context. Let me implement the feature.
Now I have enough context. Let me implement the Quick View feature.
Good, `ViewIcon` is available. Now let me implement the components:
Now let me create the product-tile override with the Quick View trigger:
Now let me check if the `react-error-boundary` package is available:
Good. Now let me verify the override resolution path works for the Quick View modal import:
Now let me also check the quick-view-modal import paths. The modal imports `ProductView` from base — that's correct. But it also imports `Link` from the base. Let me verify all imports are valid:
Good. Now let me also check the `useShopperBasketsV2MutationHelper` export from commerce-sdk-react:
Now let me verify the complete product-tile file looks correct:
I need to fix the trigger visibility — it should always be in DOM (for E2E testability) but visually revealed on hover:

Now let me also check how the `quick-view-modal` import for `useAddToCartModalContext` works. Looking at the ProductView source — it uses `useAddToCartModalContext` internally. My QuickViewContent also calls it. But I'm NOT passing `addToCart` to ProductView so ProductView won't try to open the AddToCartModal itself. My code opens it directly after the basket mutation succeeds.  Let me also verify I'm not getting into a double-render situation with `useDerivedProduct`. Since I don't pass `addToCart` to ProductView, it won't render the internal button — so its internal `useDerivedProduct` won't conflict with mine. They'll just compute the same state independently.  Now let me check that the `ModalCloseButton` positioning is correct. In the base `ProductViewModal`, `ModalCloseButton` is inside `ModalContent` directly. Let me check if placing it inside `ModalHeader` works:

Now let me verify there are no SSR issues. My component: 1. The trigger uses `isMounted` pattern ✅ 2. The modal only mounts when `isOpen` (client-side only) ✅ 3. No `window`/`document` access outside guards ✅ 4. `useProductViewModal` uses `useToast` inside — but it's only called inside the modal which is client-only ✅  Let me also check if there's an issue with the `ErrorBoundary` import. The product-tile override imports from the base which could trigger SSR-time `require` of `react-error-boundary`:
Good. Now let me check the `useBreakpointValue` import — since it's used in product-tile during SSR, I need to verify it works server-side:
Let me do a quick syntax check of the new files:
Both files parse correctly. Now let me write the implementation-status output file:
Now let me run the SSR safety checklist:
Good — no unguarded browser API access. Let me also verify there's no `Date.now()` or `Math.random()`:
Now let me commit the changes:
The commit was successful. Now let me report completion:

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me check the existing test setup and Jest configuration:
Good, `@testing-library/react` is available. Let me check how existing tests in the retail-react-app are structured to follow the same patterns:
Now I have enough context. Let me check what `useProductViewModal`, `useDerivedProduct`, and `useShopperBasketsV2MutationHelper` export signatures look like:
Now I have enough context to write the tests. Let me create the test directory structure and write comprehensive unit tests:
Now let me create the ProductTile test:
Now let me run the tests to see if they pass:
I need to handle the module resolution issue. Let me create a custom `renderWithProviders` or mock the problematic module:
I need to add a moduleNameMapper for the missing module. Let me write a custom render helper instead to avoid the dependency issue:
I need to import `@testing-library/jest-dom` for the matchers. Let me check how it's set up:
No setup file for jest-dom matchers. I need to import it in the test files. Let me add the import:
All 16 QuickViewModal tests pass. Now let's run the ProductTile tests:
All 8 ProductTile tests pass with 100% coverage. Now let's run both test suites together:
All 24 tests pass. Let me now commit the changes:
Tests are committed. Let me report success:

The unit tests for the product-quick-view feature are complete:  **QuickViewModal tests (16 passing):** - Rendering: modal visibility, required data-testids, `showDeliveryOptions={false}`, loading spinner - Add to Cart button: enabled/disabled states (variant selected, out of stock, master product, fetching) - Add to Cart flow: successful basket mutation → AddToCartModal opens → modal closes; failure keeps modal open - View Full Details link: non-empty text, correct PDP URL - Inventory message: shown/hidden based on stock state - Close button: data-testid presence  **ProductTile tests (8 passing, 100% coverage):** - Base ProductTile renders correctly - Quick View trigger appears with `quick-view-trigger-{productId}` pattern after hydration (isMounted SSR safety) - Trigger has correct aria-label - Click opens modal, event propagation stopped - Skeleton export works - Graceful handling of missing productId / alternate id field

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now let me check the existing e2e tests to match style conventions:
Now let me check the playwright config:
Let me check if `js-yaml` is available as a dependency:
Good, `js-yaml` is available. Now I have all the information I need to write the test. Let me create the dedicated test file:
Now let me run the self-review gates to ensure compliance:
The first result is a false positive (comment text mentioning "networkidle"), and the second seems odd. Let me verify:
The TS errors are from running `tsc` without a tsconfig (Playwright handles its own compilation with `esModuleInterop`). But let me fix the imports to be universally safe:
Now let me verify the file compiles and run the final self-review:
TypeScript compiles cleanly. Let me do a final verification of the file content:
Now let me commit the changes:
The commit succeeded. Let me report the outcome:

The e2e-author task is complete. I created `/workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts` with 6 test cases mapping 1:1 to the acceptance contract's `required_flows`:  1. **open-quick-view-from-tile** — Navigates to PLP, clicks Quick View trigger, asserts modal + ProductView visible 2. **switch-color-swatch-in-quick-view** — Opens modal, clicks second color swatch, verifies ProductView remains stable 3. **add-to-bag-from-quick-view** — Opens modal, clicks Add to Cart, verifies global AddToCartModal confirmation appears 4. **close-quick-view-restores-focus** — Opens modal, clicks close button, verifies trigger re-appears 5. **no-pickup-ui-in-quick-view** — Opens modal, asserts delivery-options and store-pickup are NOT visible 6. **add-to-bag-disabled-when-unavailable** — Opens modal on master product, asserts Add to Cart button is disabled  All tests include: - `dismissOverlays(page)` helper (§19) - `awaitHydrated(page)` gate (§22) - Three-outcome crash detection (§12) - Baseline-derived `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` from `baseline.json` (§17) - Console error budget assertions - Fixture-loaded URLs via `loadFixture()` (§23) - Strict-mode-safe locators with `.first()` for multi-instance testids (§20)

items.label: storefront-debug
messages: Now let me read the acceptance contract and understand what testids are expected, then look at the current implementation.
Now let me look at the actual implementation files to understand what exists:
Good, I can see the product-tile override and quick-view-modal exist. Let me read them:

I can see the issue clearly. The product tile component uses `data-testid={`quick-view-trigger-${productId}`}` (with product ID suffix), but the E2E tests and acceptance contract expect `data-testid="quick-view-trigger"` (bare form). Since the acceptance contract declares `cardinality: many`, the bare form is permitted per §7.  Let me also check if the base `ProductView` has a `data-testid="product-view"` or if we need to add a wrapper:
Good. The base `ProductView` already has `data-testid="product-view"`. The dev server is running. Let me verify quickly in the browser that the issue is what I think it is:
I can see the "Quick view" buttons in the DOM. Let me check their actual `data-testid` attributes:

Confirmed. The testids are all suffixed with product IDs (`quick-view-trigger-25752235M`, etc.) but the E2E test uses `getByTestId('quick-view-trigger')` which requires exact match and finds nothing.  The fix is clear: the acceptance contract declares `testid: quick-view-trigger` with `cardinality: many`, meaning the bare form is the correct testid. I need to change the product tile to use bare `data-testid="quick-view-trigger"`.
Let me verify the change:
Good. Now let me also check if `useDerivedProduct` is correctly imported — the import path could be another issue:
Now let me verify the fix by navigating in the browser (the dev server should hot-reload):
The fix works — 13 triggers now have `data-testid="quick-view-trigger"`. Let me also test clicking one to make sure the modal opens:

The Quick View modal opens correctly. I can see: - The modal dialog appears with product details (Checked Silk Tie) - `ProductView` is rendered inside (swatches, quantity, price, image gallery) - "Add to Cart" button is present (disabled for out-of-stock) - "View Full Details" link is present - Close button is present  Now let me check a few things about the testids inside the modal to make sure they match what the E2E tests expect:

All required testids are present in the modal: - ✅ `quick-view-modal` — found - ✅ `quick-view-modal-close-btn` — found - ✅ `product-view` — found (from base ProductView) - ✅ `quick-view-add-to-cart-btn` — found - ✅ `quick-view-view-full-details-link` — found - ✅ `inventory-message` — found (product is out of stock) - `quick-view-modal-error` — not found (expected — no error state currently)  The only issue was the testid mismatch on the trigger. Now let me run the actual E2E tests to verify the fix:

Good progress — 4 tests pass now, 2 still fail. Let me investigate both remaining failures:  1. **switch-color-swatch-in-quick-view**: can't find `data-testid="color-swatch"` 2. **add-to-bag-from-quick-view**: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'imageGroups')` in `AddToCartModal`

Found the crash bug. The `itemsAdded` array in our modal passes `{productId, quantity}` but `AddToCartModal` expects `{product, variant, quantity}` with full objects. Let me also check the swatch component:

I need to fix two issues: 1. **`itemsAdded` format crash** — pass full `{product, variant, quantity}` objects to `onAddToCartModalOpen` 2. **Missing `color-swatch` testid** — the base Swatch component doesn't have it; need to create an override  Let me fix both:
Now I need to check how the PWA Kit overrides system resolves component paths. Let me check the overrides configuration:

The SwatchGroup uses `React.cloneElement` and `children.props.value`, so wrapping in a Box would break it. Let me take a different approach — override Swatch to add testid directly to its rendered Button:
Now fix the `itemsAdded` crash in the quick view modal:
Good. Now let me verify both fixes in the browser:
Let me check if the swatch override is being applied:
The dev server may need a restart to pick up the new override file. Let me restart it:
Good, server is down. Now let me restart it:
Good, server is up. Let me verify the swatch override is working:
The swatch override is working — 15 color swatches have `data-testid="color-swatch"` on the PLP. Let me also verify they work inside the quick view modal:
I can see the modal with color swatches (Cobalt, Navy, Yellow). Let me verify the color-swatch testids in the modal:
Both fixes are working. Now let me run the E2E tests again:

The swatch override adds `color-swatch` to ALL swatches site-wide (PLP + modal). The test uses `page.getByTestId('color-swatch').nth(1)` without scoping to the modal, so it picks a PLP swatch behind the modal overlay. Let me take a different approach — remove the global override and add testids only within the modal context:
Now I'll add a `useEffect` in the QuickViewContent to tag swatches with the testid after render:
Now I need to add the ref and effect to `QuickViewContent`. Let me find the right location:
I need to add a ref for the product view container and a useEffect to tag swatches. Let me add it after the existing state hooks:
Now I need to wrap the ProductView area with the ref. Let me find the ProductView rendering and wrap it:
I need to add `ref={productViewRef}` to the `<Box>` wrapping the ProductView:
Let me verify the changes:

Good. Now I need to handle the second failure - the add-to-bag test. The error was:  ``` Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden) ```  These 403 errors are NOT being filtered by the baseline noise patterns. The baseline has `r: 403 Forbidden` as a persistent pattern, but the console error text is `Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)`. Let me check if this pattern matches:
The 403 console errors aren't matching the baseline noise patterns. Let me check the actual baseline JSON:

Good news: the `add-to-bag` test now passes the add-to-cart flow (no crash!). The only failure is the console error budget assertion catching `403 Forbidden` baseline noise that's not in the BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS. That's a `test-code` issue — the baseline didn't tag those entries as persistent.  Let me restart the server (removed the global swatch override, added modal-scoped approach) and run the tests:

The switch-color-swatch test PASSES now! The `open-quick-view` failure is a transient hydration timeout from the server restart (HMR hot-update.json 404). Let me verify `__APP_HYDRATED__` is correctly set:
Good, the flag is correctly set. The failure was transient (first request after server restart). Let me run the tests one more time to confirm the hydration failure is transient:

5/6 tests pass now. Only `add-to-bag-from-quick-view` fails, and it fails on the console error budget assertion — the 403 baseline noise issue. This is a test-code issue (the BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS in the E2E spec doesn't include `Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)` because it wasn't tagged as `persistent` in the baseline JSON).  Let me now commit my code fixes and write the debug notes:
Now let me commit the code changes:

Changes committed. Now let me report the outcome. I fixed 3 code defects that took tests from 0/6 passing to 5/6 passing. The remaining 1 failure is a test-code issue (the E2E test's baseline noise pattern doesn't cover a 403 console error that exists in the baseline but wasn't tagged as persistent).

items.label: storefront-debug
messages: Now let me read the spec, acceptance contract, and current code state:
Let me check the prior debug notes and the dev server status:
Let me check the actual git state and verify if the prior debug fixes were committed:
Let me reproduce the issue in the browser and check the e2e-runner's last report:
I can see "Quick view" buttons in the DOM! Let me verify the `data-testid` attribute is present:
The quick-view triggers ARE rendering correctly (13 found). Let me verify the full flow by clicking one:
The Quick View modal is working correctly. Let me verify all the data-testid attributes:
All testids are present and the feature is working. Now let me run the actual E2E tests to verify everything passes:
5/6 tests pass. The only failure is the console error budget in `add-to-bag-from-quick-view`. Let me look at the test to confirm this is a test-code issue:

The code defect is verified as fixed. The remaining failure is purely a test-code issue — the E2E test's `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` doesn't filter Chrome's `"Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"` messages because the baseline didn't tag them as `volatility: persistent`. Let me verify this regex mismatch:

Confirmed: the regex `/r: 403 Forbidden/` does NOT match Chrome's `"Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"`. The application code is working correctly — this is a test-code issue. Let me write debug notes and complete.
Now commit and report outcome:

The storefront code defect has been verified as **resolved**. Here's a summary:  ## What was done  1. **Verified the prior fix** (commit `1d79611f`) — the code defect from cycle 1 (quick-view-trigger not rendering) is fully resolved. All 13 product tiles show the Quick View trigger button with correct `data-testid="quick-view-trigger"`.  2. **Browser reproduction confirmed** — navigated to `/category/mens-accessories` via Playwright MCP, clicked the Quick View trigger, and verified all required DOM elements render correctly:    - `quick-view-trigger` ✅ (13 instances)    - `quick-view-modal` ✅    - `quick-view-modal-close-btn` ✅    - `quick-view-add-to-cart-btn` ✅ (correctly disabled for OOS)    - `quick-view-view-full-details-link` ✅    - `product-view` ✅    - `inventory-message` ✅  3. **E2E verification** — 5/6 tests pass. The sole remaining failure (`add-to-bag-from-quick-view`) is a **test-code issue**: the `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` regex `/r: 403 Forbidden/` doesn't match Chrome's `"Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"` (parentheses mismatch). Routed via `next_failure_hint` to `e2e-author`.

nextFailureHint.summary: The add-to-bag-from-quick-view test fails on console error budget assertion (line 282). BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS includes /r: 403 Forbidden/ but Chrome emits 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)' which uses parentheses around Forbidden and doesn't match. Add /Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 \(Forbidden\)/ to BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS.

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me look at the existing test files:

Now I have a clear picture of the issues. Let me fix both test files:  1. **product-tile test**: expects `quick-view-trigger-${productId}` but implementation uses just `quick-view-trigger` 2. **quick-view-modal test**: the `onOpen` assertion expects `itemsAdded` with top-level `productId`, but implementation passes `{product, variant, quantity}` objects

Now fix the quick-view-modal test — the issue is in the `onOpen` assertion where it expects `itemsAdded` to have a top-level `productId` but the actual implementation passes `{product, variant, quantity}`:
Now let's run the tests to verify they pass:
The error boundary test needs fixing — `ErrorBoundary` catches errors from children (like `ProductView`), not from hooks in the same component. Let me fix the approach to throw from inside the child:
Product tile tests pass (8/8). The quick-view-modal tests have a different error. Let me see the full output:
The variable must be prefixed with `mock` to be accessible in `jest.mock()`. Let me fix this:
All 17 tests pass. Now let's run both test suites together to confirm everything is green:
All 25 tests pass across both test suites (2 suites, 0 failures). Now let me commit and report:
All tests pass and changes are committed. Let me report the outcome:

Unit tests for the product-quick-view feature are now passing (25/25 tests, 2 suites). The fixes I made:  1. **ProductTile test** (`product-tile/__tests__/index.test.js`): Fixed assertions that expected `quick-view-trigger-${productId}` to match the actual implementation which uses just `quick-view-trigger` as the data-testid.  2. **QuickViewModal test** (`quick-view-modal/__tests__/index.test.js`):     - Fixed the "Add to Cart success" assertion — the implementation passes `{product, variant, quantity}` in `itemsAdded` (not a flat object with top-level `productId`). Updated the `expect.objectContaining` to match the nested structure.    - Added a proper ErrorBoundary test using a `mockShouldThrow` flag (with `mock` prefix for Jest scope rules) that makes the `ProductView` child throw during render, triggering the boundary.

items.label: e2e-author
messages: Now let me check for a triage handoff and existing tests:
Let me read the triage handoff and the existing spec file:

Now I clearly understand the issue. The triage handoff says: - **triageDomain**: `test-code`   - **triageReason**: "The test's BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS regex array fails to filter the pre-existing 403 Forbidden console errors that are documented baseline noise, causing the empty-array assertion to fail with no new application evidence."  The failing error text is `"Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"` and the existing pattern `/r: 403 Forbidden/` doesn't match it because: 1. The literal substring "r: 403 Forbidden" doesn't exist in "Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"  2. "403" is preceded by "of " not "r: ", and "Forbidden" is wrapped in parentheses  The fix is to add the full pattern that matches the actual console error. Let me make the targeted edit:
Let me use bash to make this edit:
Let me verify the change was applied correctly:
Good. Now let me run the self-review greps per the mandatory checks:
The "networkidle" match is just in a comment (JSDoc). Let me verify:
All clear. The `networkidle` match is only in a JSDoc comment, and `consoleErrors` is used 9 times (in every test). Now let me commit:
Commit successful. Now let me report the outcome:

The fix is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:  **Problem:** The `add-to-bag-from-quick-view` test was failing because the `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` regex array didn't filter the pre-existing 403 Forbidden console errors. The existing pattern `/r: 403 Forbidden/` (derived from the baseline entry `"r: 403 Forbidden"` with `volatility: "persistent"`) could never match the actual runtime error message `"Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 (Forbidden)"` because: 1. The literal substring `r: 403 Forbidden` never appears in that message ("403" follows "of " not "r: ") 2. "Forbidden" is wrapped in parentheses `(Forbidden)` in the actual message  **Fix:** Added the full pattern `/Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 403 \(Forbidden\)/` with properly escaped parentheses to the `BASELINE_NOISE_PATTERNS` array. This is a targeted, surgical fix that only modifies the failing assertion path — all 5 previously-passing tests remain byte-for-byte identical.

items.label: qa-adversary
messages: Now I have all the context. Let me create the adversarial Playwright spec and run it.
Now let me run the Playwright tests:

The two mobile probes failed due to the consent dialog blocking clicks on mobile viewport. This is a test infrastructure issue — the `dismissOverlays` helper doesn't work well at 375px width. Let me fix the mobile overlay dismissal and re-run just the failed probes:
The consent dialog overlay completely blocks the mobile viewport. Let me use a more direct approach — force-dismiss via DOM manipulation before the click:
Both mobile probes now pass. Let me run the full suite once more to get the final comprehensive results:
All 11 probes pass. Now let me write the QA report:
All probes passed. Let me report the outcome:

## Summary  **QA Adversary found no contract violations across 11 probes.**  ### Probes Executed  | # | Probe | Flow | Result | |---|-------|------|--------| | 1 | Double-click trigger (race condition) | open-quick-view-from-tile | ✅ Pass | | 2 | Keyboard-only open (Tab/Enter) | open-quick-view-from-tile | ✅ Pass | | 3 | Mobile viewport (375×800) | open-quick-view-from-tile | ✅ Pass | | 4 | Double-click color swatch (race) | switch-color-swatch-in-quick-view | ✅ Pass | | 5 | Double-click Add to Cart (double-submit) | add-to-bag-from-quick-view | ✅ Pass | | 6 | Escape key close | close-quick-view-restores-focus | ✅ Pass | | 7 | Back-button / re-entry | close-quick-view-restores-focus | ✅ Pass | | 8 | Mobile no-pickup UI | no-pickup-ui-in-quick-view | ✅ Pass | | 9 | Add to Cart disabled (master product) | add-to-bag-disabled-when-unavailable | ✅ Pass | | 10 | DOM cardinality checks | required_dom | ✅ Pass | | 11 | Open → close → re-open (state leak) | open-quick-view-from-tile | ✅ Pass |  ### Key findings - **No forbidden console patterns** (`Failed to fetch product`, `QuickView.*unmounted`) detected - **No forbidden network failures** on product/basket API endpoints - **No uncaught exceptions** (TypeError/ReferenceError/RangeError) - **Double-click race conditions** handled correctly — no duplicate modals or double-submits - **Keyboard accessibility** works — Enter activates the trigger - **Mobile viewport** renders correctly with all required DOM elements present - **Modal lifecycle** is clean — open/close/reopen has no state leaks - **Baseline noise** properly filtered using mechanical derivation from `baseline.json`
